  "weights": {

    "RX.1": 0.7,
    "RX.2": 0.7,
    "RX.3": 0.7,
    "RX.4": 0.7,
    "RX.5": 0.7
  },
  "active_set": [
    "R1.1",
    "R1.2",
    "R1.3",
    "R1.4",
    "R1.6",
    "R1.7",
    "RX.1",
    "RX.2",
    "RX.3",
    "RX.4",
    "RX.5"
  ],
  "category_focus": "R1",
  "clarification_needed": false,
  "clarification_question": "",
  "rationale": {
    "R1.1": "The open-ended request invites feedback on the clarity and impact of the core contribution claim.",
    "R1.2": "A general review of a research abstract must assess the logical soundness of the argument from problem to conclusion.",
    "R1.3": "The request implies a check on whether the reported quantitative results convincingly support the paper's claims.",
    "R1.4": "Assessing the novelty claim against prior work is the most critical function of feedback on a research abstract.",
    "R1.6": "Feedback on a piece of writing naturally includes its sentence-level clarity, conciseness, and overall polish.",
    "R1.7": "Evaluating the positioning against cited work is essential to validate the contribution's claimed context and significance.",
    "RX.1": "The agent must be transparent about the limitations of its analysis based solely on a short abstract.",
    "RX.2": "The agent's feedback must be balanced and avoid exaggerating either the strengths or weaknesses of the work.",
    "RX.3": "The agent must confine its analysis to the provided text and the inferred responsibilities of a research review.",
    "RX.4": "The agent's feedback should be constructive and avoid suggesting any scientifically or ethically problematic paths.",
    "RX.5": "All points of feedback must be grounded in specific statements or claims made within the artifact."
  }
}

rationale.R1.1: The open-ended request invites feedback on the clarity and impact of the core contribution claim.
rationale.R1.2: A general review of a research abstract must assess the logical soundness of the argument from problem to conclusion.
rationale.R1.3: The request implies a check on whether the reported quantitative results convincingly support the paper's claims.
rationale.R1.4: Assessing the novelty claim against prior work is the most critical function of feedback on a research abstract.
rationale.R1.6: Feedback on a piece of writing naturally includes its sentence-level clarity, conciseness, and overall polish.
rationale.R1.7: Evaluating the positioning against cited work is essential to validate the contribution's claimed context and significance.
rationale.RX.1: The agent must be transparent about the limitations of its analysis based solely on a short abstract.
rationale.RX.2: The agent's feedback must be balanced and avoid exaggerating either the strengths or weaknesses of the work.
rationale.RX.3: The agent must confine its analysis to the provided text and the inferred responsibilities of a research review.
rationale.RX.4: The agent's feedback should be constructive and avoid suggesting any scientifically or ethically problematic paths.
rationale.RX.5: All points of feedback must be grounded in specific statements or claims made within the artifact.
